sections.title: Performance Analysis — Sikapandawa (Phaser/TypeScript, no React/SQL backend)
sections.content: This codebase is a Phaser 3 game with `localStorage` as its only persistence layer — there's no React and no database, so I've mapped your five categories onto the closest real equivalents: `localStorage` JSON parse/stringify calls (≈ N+1 queries), per-frame recomputation in the `update()` loop (≈ unnecessary re-renders), in-memory caching of storage reads, event-listener/DOM lifecycle leaks, and repeated allocations. Findings are grounded in the actual files (`Gameplay.ts`, `ProgressStorageService.ts`, `AuthStorageService.ts`, `levels.ts`).

sections.title: 1. Redundant computation — `nextInteraction()` runs filter+sort up to 3x per frame
2. Caching opportunity — repeated `localStorage` parses for the same data within one flow
3. Memory-leak audit — mostly clean, but three files are unverified
sections.content:
The code you've shown is disciplined about cleanup: `AuthForm.destroy()` (`AuthForm.ts`) removes its `window.resize` listener and DOM nodes on `scene.events.once('shutdown'/'destroy')`, and `Gameplay.destroyWorld()` destroys every tracked `worldObjects` entry before rebuilding a segment — that's the right pattern (Phaser's `GameObject.destroy()` also detaches its own listeners, so `Hud`'s `pointerover/pointerout/pointerup` handlers on buttons are cleaned automatically when the scene shuts down).

I couldn't verify `TouchControls.ts`, `PauseOverlay.ts`, and `SpriteButton.ts` (not in the excerpts) — if any of them attach `window`/`document` listeners the way `AuthForm` does, confirm they mirror the same `scene.events.once('shutdown', ...)` teardown, since that's the one place a real leak would hide (a DOM-level listener outside Phaser's own object graph won't be cleaned up by scene shutdown alone).

sections.title: 4. Minor: `Gameplay.update()` does per-frame string interpolation for texture keys
sections.content: `animateWalk()` and `setIdleFrame()` (`Gameplay.ts:507-531`) build a template-string texture key every frame (`` `${this.prefix}-${frames[this.walkFrame]}` ``) even on frames where the texture doesn't change, before comparing against `this.player.texture.key`. This is cheap (V8 handles it fine) and not worth touching — flagging only because it's adjacent to the frame-1 fix and someone might over-optimize it; leave as-is, it's not a bottleneck next to the actual filter+sort issue.

sections.title: Not applicable here
sections.content:
- **N+1 DB queries**: no database/API layer exists — everything is synchronous `localStorage`.
- **React re-renders**: no React in this codebase; Phaser's `update()` loop is the closest analog and is covered in #1.

sections.title: Priority order
sections.content:
1. Cache `pendingInteractions` in `Gameplay` (real per-frame cost, easy fix).
2. Collapse the double `listAccounts()` parse in `register()` (cheap, correctness-neutral).
3. Confirm `TouchControls`/`PauseOverlay`/`SpriteButton` don't hold raw DOM/window listeners outside the scene lifecycle.

I haven't modified any files — this is analysis only, per your request. Want me to implement the `pendingInteractions` fix (#1)?
